title: telanthophora uspantanensis
intro: Telanthophora uspantanensis, also known as the Chiapas senna, is a plant species in the mallow family that is native to Mexico. The medicinal properties of Telanthophora uspantanensis are attributed to its anthelmintic, laxative, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been utilized in traditional medicine for centuries. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat constipation, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Telanthophora uspantanensis is traditionally prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and it has also been used in folk medicine as a treatment for various health conditions.
